original: Sirens blare and warning lights spark all around you as systems fail one after the other. Oxygen and fuel bleed from your ship from a thousand cuts as the Space Leviathan opens its infernal mouth wide!\n\nPlasma tempests flare between its skyscraper-like teeth, gleaming with the fire of a million suns. The eldritch monster wails, shaking you to your core as it surges your way, devouring your ship and everyone inside in one quick, painless gulp!

textify(cee||(cee=(0,Ce.Z)([" “I’m ready to go.” Paulette nods. “Alright. Let me just grab some things,” she says. The former XO reaches for her armor, arm cannon, and helmet. She puts them on one at a time, checking if her calibrations and settings are correct. In the meantime, Dabbebieth puts some old grav-cuffs around his head and presses the activation button. In the blink of an eye, the little leyak soars freely in the air, unbothered by gravity. “I almost feel like I’m on Mome — if the conditioned atmosphere was slightly damper, I’d be back home.” “But then my arms and legs would malfunction,” Paulette retorts. She fidgets with her headpiece’s tracking systems a little before welcoming her friend on her left shoulder. “You’re coming with us?” Dabbebieth nods. “You were there for me aboard the Gloaming and when Lithium Flower came for us — it’s only fair that I’m here for you today. I’m a bit scared of what we’ll find in the E.G.G. building, but I must be strong for you, and for [pc.name].” “That’s the spirit,” you reply. “Everyone’s ready?” Paulette gives you a thumbs up and Dabbebieth raises a tentacle. “Well then, let’s go!” Without further ado, you leave Paulette’s room and head outside. The former XO stops you on your way to the taxi terminal. “Where are you going?” “The facility is on the other side of the Gyre. We should take a cab.” “And get spotted by the zaibatsu’s spies? I know a faster and safer way,” your crewmate replies. “Which is?” Paulette unfolds her hardlight wings[pc.hasWings | and gives you her most charming smile. “Through the sky of course! Wanna race?” You answer her by deploying your [pc.wings]. She activates her Jumper shield’s thrusters in response, rising high above with a couple of flaps. You join her side a few seconds later before soaring through the Gyre. |[pc.hasJetpack | and gives you her most charming smile. “Through the sky of course! Wanna race?” You answer her by turning your jetpack on. She activates her Jumper shield’s thrusters in response, rising high above with a couple of flaps. You join her side a few seconds later before soaring through the Gyre. | and opens her arms invitingly. “Through the sky of course!” You shrug. Paulette tugs you tight against her before activating her Jumper shield’s thrusters and soaring through the Gyre on wide, bat-like hardlight wings. ] ] Cool gusts caress your cheeks as you glide along the skyscrapers. The upper air currents keep you high up the mean streets, the hovercar roads, and the choking fog, leaving you free to enjoy the technicolor landscape that is Dhaal’s dark hemisphere. Up above, you notice vehicles and spaceships docking on the Meridian’s buildings — certainly rich offworlders or high ranking Peers. It feels so alien to travel on the edge of these two unforgiving worlds: the political hellscape birthed by the zaibatsu’s power struggle up above and the struggle to live another day in a polluted, cyberpunk city down under. Having been to both sides on your quest, you don’t know which is the deadliest of the two. “When I was a little girl, I told my mother and sisters that when I grew up, I’ll fly high, that I’d reach [silly |the highest of heavens |the stars ] and look down on the Peers. I did fly high, higher than most zaika have, visited worlds we couldn’t begin to imagine, made friends with beings from across the cosmos, and yet I still feel shackled. As if I didn’t break all my chains. Even now, [silly |flying in the freedom |as free as the wind that blows ], I feel something weighing me down.” Dabbebieth hangs from Paulette’s shoulder. “You sure it’s not just me? I’m heavier than I look,” the little leyak impishly teases. Paulette giggles. “I’m used to you by now, featherweight.” “Whatever stands in our way, we’ll break through it[silly | all ]! Nothing can stop the three of us,” you reply. The purple cyborg nods. “Right. [var0 |Last one to reach the coordinates owes a drink to the others!” |Buckle up friends! This will be a long, bumpy flight!” ] "]))),[function(){return pc.hasJetpack()||pc.hasWings()}]
Next

textify(See||(See=(0,Ce.Z)([" “Look! A terminal,” you say, pointing at a sinister holoscreen. The glowing green display flickers weakly, fighting for life as its battery slowly runs out of power... “Good call,” Paulette replies. She kneels next to it and opens her left hand. In the blink of an eye, multi-purpose cables couple with the terminal’s port, linking it to the former XO’s systems. “Let’s see... Stars! This place is ten floors high and has its own fusion reactor! I wonder why it’s offline and they’re drawing from the city instead? It isn’t damaged at all, but for some reason, it’s disconnected from the grid.” Weird... maybe someone sabotaged it to better raid the place. “Can you turn it back on?” Dabbebieth asks. “It’s too dark and somber here.” Paulette nods. “Yes, but I need security clearance. Pass the badge on the panel.” On the one hand, you’ll see what you’re dealing with. On the other hand, there’s no way your presence will go unnoticed now. If there’s security droids or turrets, they’ll be back on as well. “We dealt with the T4L0Ns in the dark, we’ll make it,” Paulette retorts. The shy leyak presents the magnetic badge to the holo device. A big Access Granted lights the dying screen a moment before the orange neon shuts down. You stay in the dark for a couple of seconds before bright, white lights fill the room with warm colors. The leyak-controlled machines you defeated look even bigger and scarier now that you can see the full extent of their modified frames. Sharp syringes dripping with green liquids line vicious, spider-like legs linked to a central aquarium protecting their mindwashed drivers. Long, tentacular cables gather the trapped leyaks’ venom while big tubes inoculate them with dark fluids. What evil spirit corrupted these poor, famished souls? “We’ll save you,” Dabbebieth promises. “Friends, I think I made a terrible mistake!” Paulette shouts. What’s going on? The purple cyborg grimaces. “Remember when I said the building had ten floors? It only has nine.” So what’s the la-- Suddenly, the ground starts shaking. You hold onto a collapsed support beam, hugging it tightly as the quakes come stronger and faster. “Main functions restored. Processing last orders. Mag-binds: released. Avionics: online. Reactor: nominal. Thrust: 63%, increasing. Initiating stabilizers,” a computerized voice barks through damaged loudspeakers. Wait! Reactor, avionics, thrusters — this isn’t a building! “We’re aboard a spaceship!” you exclaim. Dabbebieth gawks in shock. “Impossible!” Paulette replies. “The Peers never spent a dime on rocket tech! They wanted us bound to Dhaal, without any hope of escaping them!” Well, someone clearly had other plans. You withstand the crushing force of gravity for a long, painful minute before the building stops shaking. You weakly stumble toward Paulette, falling on your knees next to her. The former XO does her best to navigate the network through her wireless connection, gathering as much data as she can from the derelict computer. “Stars above, you were right! We are aboard the Extraplanetary Guardian Galleon, a zaika-made ship! According to my readings, it was designed in secret by the Peer in charge of the E.G.G. project: Doctor Ovvi... [silly |Positorii |Hellitrix ],” she mumbles, struggling to pronounce the scientist’s name. She’s shaking, eyes lost in thought and time. “Paulette, do you hear me? Paulette!” The former XO snaps out of her trance. “S-Sorry. An uneasy memory came back to haunt me.” “Do you know this Dr. [silly |Positorii |Hellitrix ]?” Dabbebieth asks. “I’m... not sure. I remember Lithium Flower wrathfully screaming her name. I remember carrying heavy stuff at the time, but everything else is a blur. I’m sorry.” You [pc.isAss |shrug, placing a hand on her shoulder |give her a comforting hug ]. “It’s fine. You’ll remember in due time. For now, let’s focus on controlling the ship. The helm must be here somewhere.” Before you can say more, the robotic voice booms through the loud hailers again. “All systems [silly |full power! |online! ] Destination: The Crucible!” “The Crucible?” Paulette frowns. “According to the navigation, it’s Dhaal’s biggest active volcano. It’s in the middle of the sun-scarred hemisphere, surrounded by an ocean of its own lava. I don’t know why the ship needs molten metal and I don’t want to know why. Let’s stop it before it’s too late! According to the plans, the helm is south of here. With some luck, Strict-9’s clearance will suffice to hijack the flying fortress.” "])))
Next

textify(xee||(xee=(0,Ce.Z)([" You dust the abandoned crate off in the hope to get some data about its contents. Unfortunately, there’s only a worn out Paragon logo on it. Guess you’ll be in for a surprise. Without a warning, Paulette rips the top off. The former XO screeches, leaping out of reach. You quickly take a fighting stance, ready to fight whatever abomination lies inside. Your [pc.eyes] go wide as you discover a dozen of perfectly-smooth spheres. Feeling adventurous, you take one, raising it up to see it in plain light. There’s a sort of push up button mechanism installed inside it and a big lightning bolt symbol on top of it. “Be careful with that [pc.name]! Paragon’s stun grenades are brutal.” “How brutal are we talking?” The zaika gulps. “XO-busting brutal, and I’m not nearly as well-shielded. One pulse and my systems will be down for at least a minute — assuming I don’t take a direct hit. I’ve heard stories of a full cohort being neutralized thanks to a lucky shot.” Well, if the leyak cyborgs or whatever monstrositues lurking into this factory threaten you again, you know how you’ll greet them. "])))
Next

textify(Iee||(Iee=(0,Ce.Z)([" [flags.EGG_T4L0N |[var0 |You load the program and smile as you are shuffled to an artificial reality on a current of perfectly programmed electrons. You find yourself back in time, a few moments before being jumped by the cybernetic leyaks. ]Something clicks on the left. Paulette raises her arm cannon, pointing laser sights and searchlights at the source. “[pc.name], did you hear that?” “Yes. Where are they?” “Everywhere!” In the blink of an eye, two — no, three T4L0Ns emerge out of the vents in the walls and the ground! They slowly stalk closer to you, weapons at the ready! Another set falls out of the ceiling, separating Paulette from you - she has her own problems to deal with! “Snap out of it!” Dabbebieth shouts. “You are proud Hik’uhms, not some machine slaves! Please!” Paulette grunts. “It’s no use: they are in a hypnotic trance. If we damage their exoskeletons, maybe it’ll snap them out of it!” |You’ve got a bad feeling. You can’t put your finger on it, as if someone’s watching you from the shadows, baring its fangs, ready to strike. “[pc.name] — something’s moving in the dark.” Dabbebieth floats over you. The little leyak moves erratically, as if he were trying to pinpoint the source of an echo. “I can feel it. No, them! They are all around us, all across the building! Hundreds of them!” Something clicks on the left. Paulette raises her arm cannon, pointing laser sights and searchlights at the source. A fresh plasma cut disfigured the wall! You start, feeling something roll next to you, missing you by a hair’s breadth. It went so fast! You didn’t see it. “No! It can’t be! What have they done to you!” Dabbebieth wails, his voice loaded with sorrow and rage. The things circle you, making their lithe, bladed limbs clink on the metallic ground. They are about 2' tall with a large, spherical core and four — no, five limbs: two on each side and a segmented stinger at the center. The darkness gives their somber frames a striking advantage against the three of you. Suddenly, the orange neons flare, highlighting your assailants! You immediately recognize your opponents: leyaks! They look a lot like your crewmate but with larger sails. Someone surgically augmented them to interface with long, insectile stalks. Tubes and electrodes link the robotic parts to their scalps, keeping the poor aliens under control. A bold, white T4LON is embroidered on their front left leg — that must be their designation. “Snap out of it!” Dabbebieth shouts. “You are proud Hik’uhms, not some machine slaves! Please!” Paulette grunts. “It’s no use: they are in a hypnotic trance. If we damage their exoskeletons, maybe it’ll snap them out of it!” Another group surrounds her, splitting your party into two separate confrontations! ] It’s a fight! textify(Gee||(Gee=(0,Ce.Z)([" “I’ll check the lockers and desks,” you say. “And we’ll investigate the cells,” Paulette adds, holding Dabbebieth close. The poor thing’s freezing in here! You slowly walk to the metallic furniture, starting by the largest desk. You try to open the drawers; all but one is locked. Inside, you find neatly-arranged folders, certainly medical files. Reaching inside, you pull the most recent one out and open it on the tabletop. Project T4L0Ns: Type 4 Light 0 Neoforms, subject 32. Supervisor: Dr. Ovvi [silly |Positorii |Hellitrix ]. Pictures of a captured leyak before and after the mechanisation process accompany sheets after sheets filled with medical babble. Here and there, you notice a few readable comments: rejected first implant, good mnemonic connection, relentless, clumsy, install [silly |morph ball |wheeler module ], raise rations by 23%... They tried to weaponize the alien lifeforms! You reach for another folder: Project [silly |Shadows |Umbra ]. Supervisor: Dr. Ovvi [silly |Positorii |Hellitrix ]. There’s no pictures in this one, but the formulas and cybernetics involved were carefully written down. Growth hormones, photoreactive cells, [silly |magentite extracts |gravity flux inverters ], life support system, bioregenerative alloys... some sort of bio-weapon designed to thrive outside of the Gyre. A red, underlined comment specifies that it’s extremely sensitive to erotic stimuli as a side effect of its hormonal treatment. If it gets too aroused, it’ll be vulnerable until it vents it all out. You read a little more scientific jargon before closing the folder. When you stow it, a pair of keys drop on the floor. Bingo! The drawers and lockers open one after the other, revealing their dark secrets. Most of them contain nothing but white, glossy blouses and protective goggles, but some have more interesting things to share. Opening the penultimate one, you find a galactic standard holodisk! Weird... how did it end up here? There’s nothing else in the locker and the one next to it is empty. Maybe it belonged to one of Strict-9’s prey? You take your place at the desk again and install it into your Codex. A lone file named Mission orders pops on the screen. Puzzled, you open the file and start reading. This... this is insane! According to this document, Dr. Ovvi [silly |Positorii |Hellitrix ] planned to conquer Dhaal! She implanted backdoors in her cybernetic creations and used research funds to develop shady tech behind the zaibatsu’s backs. When they found out about her real goals, they sent elimination teams to put an end to her plans. The doctor went missing, but not before releasing her T4L0Ns into the building. The factory was closed and the captured scientists were sent to the fourth-generation XOs design teams, with workers as their test subjects — well, the ones that the T4L0Ns didn’t suck dry. The last entry mentions the imminent deployment of ‘Strict-9’ and ‘Sci’Koh,’ an alien psionic, to track down the missing mad woman behind it all, reclaim the place from trespassers, and pacify the mindwashed leyaks so the zaibatsu could salvage what remained. Unfortunately, there’s no mention of access badges anywhere. Another dead end. “[pc.name]? Can you come here, quickly?” Without further ado, you join Paulette inside the medical yard’s depths. Dozens of empty and broken cells occupy this end of the room. From time to time, you notice torn cables and colorful stains on the ground. Their inhabitants must have been forcibly dragged out of the oversized test tubes. Dabbebieth waves at you from the very end of the aisle. He’s by Paulette’s side, next to a glowing stasis container. “What’s going on?” The purple cyborg points at the cell. “I think we found our runaway scientist,” she says, tapping the reinforced glass. You can see a tall silhouette through the opaque window. Maybe a survivor. “Did you find anything?” You nod. “An holodisk with orders to capture Dr. Ovvi [silly |Positorii |Hellitrix ] and neutralize the T4L0Ns. It seems to belong to a corpo agent going by the name ‘Sci’Koh.’ They must be a mercenary the zaibatsu hired to work with Strict-9 to take control of the place. There’s also bits and pieces about more experiments running loose.” The shy leyak gasps. “Oh dear.” “Did you find an access badge?” “No,” you sadly reply. Paulette sighs, turning back to the darkened tube and the feminine form within. “Then she’s our last hope.” "])))
Next

textify(Hee||(Hee=(0,Ce.Z)([" [var0 |You load the program and smile as you are shuffled to an artificial reality on a current of perfectly programmed electrons. You find yourself back in time, a few moments before meeting the vulpatra psychowarrior. ]Paulette enters the research section first, arm cannon at the ready. The place is full of experimental machines loaded with savicite crystals floating in a purple concoction. [pc.cl kineticist |They look a lot like the ones you were placed into when Dad discovered your psionic potential. Your skull itches looking at the electrode-lined headgear. To this day, you remember the cold, wet feel of the sucklers against your bare scalp. |They belong more in a horror movie than anything. Stars, Dhaal should have been paradise, not a dystopian hellhole! ] “Woah! The new stuff’s all glossy-shiny!” Zero Plus babbles. “Stay on your guard. We don’t know what- hngh!” [pc.cl kineticist |This pain, this emotional deluge, it’s all too familiar to you! Someone’s trying to sneak into your mind! |A deluge of lustful images flood your mind. Something, or someone’s trying to control you! ] Zero Plus weakly flops on the ground. Mustering your willpower, you stumble toward her and check her state. She’s in a trance, her cheerful eyes dilated into large pie plates. “W-What the- Dabbebieth! What are you doing!” Paulette shouts. You turn around. The blue leyak mercilessly whips her with her envenomed tentacles. She’s doing her best to defend herself, deflecting the toxic onslaught with her chrome — but she won’t hold on much longer! She turns desperate eyes on you, wordlessly begging you for help before firing harmless flares at her mind-controlled friend. The presence in your head batters harder and harder, hoping to break you. “Give up! Kneel! Be Free in Servitude! Be a Good [pc.mf |Boy |Girl ]! Obey!” Suddenly, you receive an image of the psionic lab, but not from your point of view. The voice is close, very close. You reach for your gear and [pc.isNaga |slither |run ] through the aisles, gritting your teeth in preparation for a rough fight. Before you can reach it however, the telepath floats out of her hiding place surrounded by defensive debris. You gawk in shock at the sight of the hovering vulpatra facing you. She’s wearing a tight bodysuit that leaves nothing to the imagination under a school girl outfit[pc.isAss | - fucking kinksters! |. ] Her G-cups bounce in the air as supernatural winds blow, lifting her skirt and revealing a sizable bulge and apple-sized balls. Her large, pussy-like ears are stuffed with a particularly weird headphone-like device. Oh dear, it has knotted savicite dildos in place of earmuffs! The psi-active toys sparkle with power as the shortstack fox channels eldritch energies into her puffball antenna. She raises her fingers, a wicked smile on her face. Without warning, an icicle flies a few inches towards you at high speed before burying itself deep inside the wall. Stars, that was close! “I didn’t expect visitors. You must be very tough if you beat Strict-9 and my cyborg pawns. Unfortunately, your little trip through the E.G.G. ends here. Soon, you’ll be added to my toys.” A telepathic whiplash strikes you square in the chest. “You won’t take us down that easily,” you shoot back. The floating vulpatra grunts. “You little pest! I’m lucky you brought the brainless nurse and the blue jellyfish with you. Without them, I’d have no chance against your GU. Their brains are completely fucked over by drugs and neuronal reprogramming... a tangled mess for us telepaths. Fortunately, I have other ways to break you!” Paulette screams as Zero Plus and Dabbebieth double down on her. The leyak shakily holds her against the wall while the bimbo doctor tries to sedate your crewmate. If she doesn’t free herself quickly, you’ll be surrounded! “It’s over, give up!” Sci’Koh wails directly into your head. “This vessel and its secrets are mine!” “[pc.isAss |I wouldn’t count on it, bitch |In your dreams ]!” It’s a fight! "]))),[function(){return e}]
Fight!

textify(tte||(tte=(0,Ce.Z)([" Thic[silly |c |k ] vines block the way to the exit. You need to find something to cut it down. Paulette scans the vegetal barrier, scouring the room with her sensors. “The whole area is overgrown. If I use my plasma weapons, we’ll risk burning alive in a forest fire of our own creation.” “Can you cut them?” Dabbebieth asks. She shakes her head. “It’s too dense to be trimmed with conventional means. We either need to damage the roots or find a super weedkiller.” “Let’s look for it then.” It’ll be hard to find something with all the wild plants and growths covering everything. You head to the closest shelf and reach for all the chemicals you can get. Zero Plus happily bounces next to you, snatching more sloshing stuff and abrasive powders while Dabbebieth and Paulette explore the other side of the biolab. You slowly gather your findings on a wooden slab. You recognize some of the compounds, but most of them are completely alien to you — and the zaika nomenclature doesn’t help. The labels don’t make any sense, either. Their version of bleach is somehow acidic, instead of being a base! “It’d be easier if we could vent the place,” you remark. The bimbo nurse tilts her head. “Like... Make the fans all blowy windy?” Yes! Does she know how? “Hum... I don’t know. The fans are full of flowers and tree-stuff! And they’re so pretty and cute!” It’s probably better to leave them that way; no telling what might get clogged if you fired it up. On the other side of the gloaming greenhouse, the former XO rummages through heavy crates. “Plasma cutter, plasma scissors, plasma weedwacker... where are the ol’ fashioned chainsaws? Wait! What’s this?” Paulette fishes a jeweled bracer from the toolbox. It’s fitted with a large, yellow stone, giving it a very stylish look — it’s not something you’d find in a botanical yard. Maybe a Peer lost her jewels during the leyak onslaught. “Gosh! Paullie, it goes so well with your eyes. You should, like, put it on.” Your crewmate sighs but complies nonetheless. She fastens the bracer around her wrist and places her hand on her chest, making her armored boobs bounce hypnotically. “So? How do I look?” “Gorgeous,” the dollified doctor giggles. “I think it’s a telly-somethingy. I saw big, tough cookies wearing them for a test, but I didn’t see them leave the place. Maybe it’s magic!” Zero Plus squeals with eyes full of glitter. Paulette tries to press the gemstone or to find a hidden switch but nothing comes out of it. Dimly lighting the room, a tiny holoscreen appears in front of her eyes. “Low Battery.” “Strange,” Dabbebieth remarks. “Maybe there’s a handbook for it somewhere?” Yeah, let’s keep looking. You’ll find a way to get through eventually. "])))
Next

textify(gte||(gte=(0,Ce.Z)([" “[pc.name]? Can we talk?” Paulette whispers. Her voice is shaky and her eyes are weary, as if she were sick — sick or frightened. You nod. “What’s the matter?” The purple cyborg sighs. “I don’t know... I thought I was strong, that this was all behind me,” she mumbles, looking at her orange limbs, “but I was wrong. I’m not strong, I’m not brave, I’m still a scared little girl, lost in a world yearning to crush her. I startle at the slightest noise, my own shadow terrifies me — if it weren’t for my XO training, I’d be crying in a corner.” No way. You try your best to reassure her. “[pc.kmhbb |It’s alright to be scared, but you aren’t alone. Remember Lithium Flower? You were afraid of her too, but together, we defeated her. I know that things look bad now and it’s exactly why we can’t give up. Just like you weren’t Lithium Flower’s toy, you aren’t a pawn from the E.G.G. You are more than a GU, and never forget it. |The big bad dragon’s scared?” you tease. “Don’t worry princess. As long as you’re with us, no one will hurt you. We’ll get through it all, defeat everyone that stands in our way and take control of this ship together. |Scared, Paulette? It doesn’t sound like you! Don’t you remember how easily we beat Lithium Flower? Don’t forget who you truly are. A simple XO or GU would already be a cum-stained pile of broken metal and stretched holes, and you know why? Because they are weak, truly weak. |Daww, I’m scared too! This place’s like, so full of whorrors. If y’all weren’t here with me, I’d be balling and quaking like crazy! |Don’t let this place get through you. Alone, you are strong, together, we are unstoppable. You aren’t a broken XO or a failed experiment — you are terrific! And that’s all you need to know. ]” The chromed zaika smiles. “I know this — it’s just... this place, it’s giving me the creeps,” she murmurs. She turns around, facing one of the big, grinning faces plastered on the walls. The smirking zaika visage looks so vile, so cruel, and its crossed out glasses leave no doubt on how little it cares about your safety. You can feel the presence of Dr. Ovvi [silly |Positorii |Hellitrix ] through them; the madness of an unbound mind unleashed on poor, desperate girls and helpless aliens. You barely saw her silhouette in the logs but you could feel her insane aura in your guts. “Everything reeks of her: the walls, the ground, the air — she cursed and poisoned everything. Even this poor girl,” she says, glancing at Zero Plus. The bimbo nurse happily bounces next to Dabbebieth on her way to the elevator shaft. Her scantily-clad assets wobble madly, barely-contained by her skintight bodysuit and swaying coat. “We’ll find a way to save her. Then, we’ll restore your memories.” A sinister light sparks in Paulette’s eyes. “What if... we leave her like this?” You blink. “D-Did you lose it? textify(bte||(bte=(0,Ce.Z)([" Did they find the key? Zero Plus shakes her head. “We found, like, so many cool thingies — but no key. The pens with the hearts or the ones with the animals?” You... aren’t sure. At least you’ll have plenty if you get hurt. Did they search the desks and lockers? “From top to bottom,” Dabbebieth answers. “I looked under, over, and even behind, but no trace of the key. Are you sure that there was one here?” The bimbo nurse nods. “Yip Yup! It had a big, red tag on it, like a rectangle.” Maybe a flash drive? Zero Plus slowly opens her mouth in a wide, round O. “You think it’s in a compy- compo- screen thingie?” The shy leyak slowly floats down. “It’s the only stuff we didn’t search for. If the key isn’t there, then it must be somewhere else.” Fortunately, there’s only one electronic machine in this section of the lab. The polished console looks old but not decrepit, as if it was installed before everything else in the room. You sit in front of the keyboard and plug your Codex in. A moment later, the green screen comes to life, asking for a password. “Up to you, Zero Plus.” “Okie Dokie!” In the blink of an eye, the dollified doctor inputs her Mistress’s credentials. The screen opens to a full desktop! There’s loads of reports, images, and data labeled in strange, incomprehensible ways. Even your Codex struggles to understand what they are about. “What now?” Dabbebieth reaches for the machine. “Zero Plus? What’s the zaika symbol for a key?” The [pc.isBimbo |smart cookie |snow-white bimbo ] giggles. “I think it’s this one and this one,” she says, pointing at two strange letters. The gelatinously buxom alien types them into the search bar and starts a widespread research of the term. “This will take some time, but it’s worth trying.” By the Fjud’hom’s maw, this place’s terrifying. You wonder how this Dr. [silly |Positorii |Hellitrix ] could work in this dark mess. Zero Plus giggles. “Well, that’s like, super easy to answer! She was the bestest at keeping stuff around. She always knew where she left her files, even if they were under piles and piles of shiny junk. Nothing could escape her for long.” That’s oddly ominous. She shrugs. “Mistress was furious that they invaded her factory-building-ship-thingie. She told me to hide from visitors and T4L0Ns and wait for her return, but she never came back. Gosh, I miss her. She was so loud, mean, and harsh, but I miss her a lot. I’m so lucky that you’ve freed me! You aren’t a T4L0N and Paullie cutie’s my big GU sis, so you must be the good guys!” Dabbebieth nods. “We’ll do everything we can to liberate everyone from these nasty tanks. I’ve been trapped inside one for months, deprived of food and sapiency, nothing but a caged pet. Just seeing bubbling tubes makes me shiver.” Before you can add anything, the search results prompt on screen. There’s exactly two files that mention keys! That narrows things down. “Let’s try the first one,” Zero Plus proposes, attracted by the vibrant image attached to it. Without further ado, you open the Governable Units report. Dabbebieth gasps, recognizing the author’s face. “Lithium Flower... Speak of the devil, it’s that hell-cat again.” Dabbebeith moans. “Oh! I remember her! She was so small and huggable! She’d look so cute with fluffy, kitty pawbsies!” Zero Plus squees. The little leyak frowns. “Maybe. Let’s see what she has to say about the key.” You dive into the document. It’s a very technical report of her work on the GU project from development to the first prototypes. Cybernetics, bio-engineering, physiological changes, implants,... she reviewed everything they fitted on their guinea pigs. It hurts to see the pictures attached. The poor girls were twisted, remodeled, and experimented on relentlessly. Some had scythes for arms, others had their lower bodies replaced with insectile legs like those equipped on the T4L0Ns, others had their arms swapped for wings and their legs replaced by vicious cannons, and so on... “There’s so many of them!” Dabbebeith gasps. Zero Plus points at the diagram in the top right corner of each GU. “What’s this?” You click on one of them. A complex chart describing the modded zaika’s abilities, strength, speed, toughness, flexibility, combat awareness, and stylishness. The higher the score, the stronger the cyborg. Amidst all of them, one doesn’t have a score — certainly because she was designed before it was introduced. Her face is barely visible under her dense, cumbersome helmet, but you know full well whose eyes are staring at you from the past. It feels so wrong to read her personal file. “Let’s move on, please. Pages upon pages of notes scroll in front of your eyes, revealing more wicked results with each test. I didn’t sign up for this! I admired her; how she rose through Bright Waters’ hierarchy before uniting all the zaibatsu under her banner for this project, but now I can see the truth behind her lies. She isn’t a scientific genius, she’s a mad goddess! Nothing’s sacred to her, not even her own body. I’ll accept Paragon’s offer and bring GU-1 with me. She can take everything from me, make my life miserable, but she won’t take her away from me!” “I’ll conquer the universe and seed the stars myself!” Zero Plus sighs. “No key here.” Next file, then. You click on the unassuming link. A small window opens and asks for a password. “Zero Plus?” The bimbo nurse giggles. Her fingers quickly dance on the keyboard again, filling the bar with stars. Once it’s full, the image flashes brightly and a secret compartment opens in the computer. Dabbebieth stretches his tendrils, fishing into the hidden vault. A moment later, he extracts a bright red flash drive from its depths. “Is it?” “The key!” Zero Plus gasps. It wasn’t hidden, just plugged into the wrong slot. Your companions cheer with you — stars, it might be a small victory, but it’s a very sweet one. “Almost there. Soon, this nightmare will be over,” Dabbebieth sighs. The loud hailers buzz over your head. “Deploying magma extractors! Attention personnel, ship stability compromised!” “Yeeeeeeeeeeeeeee! We’re gonna sway a lot!” giggles the already-wobbling bimbo. The shy leyak gives you the key. “[pc.name], we must hurry! If the pipes burst or melt, the ship’s gonna crash! We must override the computer, quickly!” "])))

Alright, time to be a good samaritan. You punch in the coordinates, redirecting your ship towards the Ice Queen. One brief LightDrive sprint later and you’re within visual range of the ship - and a hail of tumbling rocks hurtling through space. Considering how many comets are streaking by you, it looks like the Queen must have gotten caught in a storm. Even with modern shields, a hit from something big enough going fast enough can be the end of you - or do enough damage to make your equipment go haywire.

\n\nSure enough, when you zero in on the vessel, your forward cameras start picking up chunks of metal and plastic gracefully rolling through the endless void around the vessel. The Ice Queen is a big girl in her own right, even with what looks like a nasty scrape along her starboard side. She definitely took a big impact. The Queen’s all orange and red, curvaceously circling an expansive cargo bay with crew quarters, engines, and a single defensive rocket pod that automatically angles your way.

\n\nYou keep your shields up just in case, but happily, the Ice Queen doesn’t do so much as blink at you until you’re within a hundred meters. Once you’re at the stellar equivalent of bumping noses with it, a computerized alert appears on your HUD, directing you to align yourself with the Queen’s port side. You circle around and, once you’re in position, a boarding umbilical extends from it and bonks against your hull with a resounding vibration, making the hull tremble ever so slightly. A button press allows the umbillical to link up with your airlock, forming an air-tight bridge you can traverse.
\n\nSecured to the other vessel, you hop off the captain’s seat, grab your gear, and get ready to head over.

\n\nJust as you’re getting through with the mountain of legal gobbledygook the Tove sent you, your proximity sensors alert you to the approach of a large ship bearing the trade IDs of the Camarilla. You respond to a message blip ordering you to power down weapons and shields and follow the “wabeshift” to Uveto Station’s docking terminal. You do as you’re ordered and switch the autopilot on, letting it dog the Camarilla ship in towards the station.

\n\nUveto Station’s a small affair, one of the pre-fab low-orbit control stations common on frontier worlds too inhospitable to support a full colony. Why it’s in what’s been a core world for centuries, you have no idea. A thick tether connects it to the planet below - a space elevator, you imagine - and several long, curving arms extend from the central unit of the station, providing a great deal of docking space. You suppose there must not be a spaceport on the surface of the planet.

is looking out for you?

\n\nYour companion scoffs and clambers to his feet. “Can’t imagine the Camarilla actually caring about anyone, unless you’re bringing a boat-load of credits with you. Heartless little muppets only think of one thing: cold, hard credits.”

\n\nHe laughs, but you’re not sure if it’s in mirth or rue. While Zaalt stares out the forward viewscreen, you busy yourself shepherding the auto-pilot while it does the fine calculations, making sure you’re perfectly aligned in orbit before you attach to the automated umbilical.
\n\n“Alright!” Zaalt cheers as the connection locks in: you’ve finally docked. He checks the device on his wrist and growls a curse. “Cutting it damn close, [pc.name]! Gotta... Gaah!”

\n\nZaalt’s eyes go wide and he plunges forward, just barely stopping himself on the back of your seat. You leap up and grab him before he can fall to the deck, and the moment you touch his furred flesh, you’re instantly assailed by the sheer heat radiating off of him. He’s burning up!
\n\nYou ask if he’s okay - what’s wrong? - but all the response you get is a weak groan of pain. What the hell?

\n\nYou’re half way to punching the emergency comms when Zaalt manages to wave his hand at you and staggers to his feet. “I-I’m okay. I’m okay,” he groans, rubbing his temples. “Just a... just a migraine. I’ll be alright.”
\n\nMigraine? With a fever like that? Bullshit; he’s sick!

\n\nZaalt grunts and waves you off, though he’s still feverishly rubbing at his head. “Fuck this hurts. Agh. Just... don’t worry about it. Got less than half an hour to deliver these, uh... these cores. C’mon.”

\n\n“Are you sure?” you ask, following Zaalt as he forces himself towards the airlock. He gasps and slumps against the bulkhead, gritting his tusked teeth against the pain. Again, you draw near to help... but this time Zaalt slams one of his meaty paws against your chest, sending you staggering back! He roars like a beast, throwing his head back with feral outrage.

\n\nYour brain hurts. Not a headache, but a sudden rush of internal agony that makes you stumble back and scream, clawing at your skull as if to get at your brain and stave off the agonizing assault. It feels like talons are clawing at your mind, rending your senses. What’s happening to you!?
\n\n“[pc.name]!?” a voice shouts from off the bridge. “Are you okay?”
\n\nNo, you’re not okay! Not at all.

\n\nZaalt’s hands are on you before you can regain your balance, throwing you backwards against your captain’s chair. He bellows and snarls, and suddenly you have claws slicing at you, trying to tear you apart! You manage to shove him back and grab your [pc.weapon], interposing it between yourself and the crazed sabertooth. By now he’s breathing heavily, staring at you with dilated, hugely black eyes. Yours, however, are drawn downwards to a swelling bulge in his breeches, standing out like a pillar of desperate need from his loins.
He grabs the force blade from his belt and activates it, letting a blade of shimmering purple energy spring to life between you.
\n\nWith him standing between you and the corridor out, there’s nothing you can do but try and pacify him... somehow.

The Ghost

A moment later and you’re aboard the Ghost, Kara’s ship, looking around at the sleek, bright white of the interior. She’s a new ship, still squeaky clean and polished to a shine. Consoles run along the length of every corridor you pass through on the way to the bridge. The whole ship has an oddly sterile feel - not like the cozy home you’ve made out of your own ship.

\n\nAs you’re walking, a side passage in the corridor slides open with a hiss, and a pale blue beauty sashays out into your path. You blink hard as the robotic bombshell smiles at you with big, ultramarine lips, putting her hand on a set of broad hips that strain the band of her skin-tight yoga pants. Military command-grade operating system, made utterly and completely loyal after I poked around in her brain... and she’s rocking a damn fine body besides.”

\n\nThe only crew you see, however, is a brunette woman sitting in the pilot’s seat, bobbing her head beneath a headset that echoes punk-sounding music over the otherwise silent deck. “[pc.name], this is my first mate, Logan,” Kara says, reaching down to run her sharp-clawed hands through the other woman’s long brown hair. The first mate gasps in surprise and yanks her headphones off, looking over her shoulder with a pronounced scowl. “Don’t sneak up on me like that!” she snaps. “I’m still jumpy after...”

The Ghost

The trip goes by quickly and peacefully, until you hit the arid atmosphere of Myrellion. The ship shudders with turbulence, and you watch as Logan hammers several controls on a console above her. Part of the forward screen flickers off, replaced by a bird-eye view of the desert - some kind of probe’s camera, maybe? The second screen focuses in on some kind of walled compound sitting at the bottom of a deep caldera, surrounded by nothing for miles and miles around. You squint, and think you make out all of three, maybe four buildings on the surface.
\n\nNot much of a base.
\n\n“Bring us in low, Logan. Nice and easy,” Kara says casually, leaning back in her captain’s chair with legs crossed. “Any sign of Void ships?”
\n\n“None,” your pilot calls back, flipping a button that replaces your view of the base with a radar image of the surrounding airspace: totally blank.

\n\nKara nods slowly. “Alright, drop the rear hatch and bring us in. We’ll drop on that plateau, just ahead. [pc.name], you’re with me. We’re gonna fast rope onto the surface. Stealth system keeps us off their LIDAR, but doesn’t help much if we’re kicking up dust right on their front porch.”

\n\nShe hops up and grabs that silver case of hers, still closed. You follow her off the bridge and through the gently-curving alabaster corridors of her ship, all the way to the freighter’s opposite end and down a short elevator ride to the cargo deck.
Connie’s waiting there for you, standing at an almost military at-ease as the cargo ramp slides down beside her. “Best of luck, captain.”
\n\nWind whips past your face, threatening to either blast you back or suck you out with equal force.

Connie leans out with a robotic surety, securing a hook to an auto-loader cable on the roof and kicking out a long length of synth-silk rope, a hundred feet or more tumbling to the dirt. Kara tosses you a pair of climber’s gloves before grabbing on and sliding down and out of sight. You give the helpful robot a nod and grab the rope, following the Ghost’s captain down to the surface.

Kara tosses you a pair of climber’s gloves before shoving you aside and rushing past, right towards the lowering door. You have just enough time to blink before she spins on a heel, gives you a wink, and dives backwards out the door.

\n\nIt takes you a moment to realize there’s a huge bundle of synth-silk rope training after her, suddenly going taut as she lands. You let out a breath you hadn’t realized you were holding and grab it, following her down.

Kara’s sitting beside it, wrapped up in a blue bathrobe with her legs crossed under her, sipping at a steaming mug. “Sleep well? I did.”

You quietly sit down at the familiar table in the back corner of the Golden Peak, across from your lover. She doesn’t look up from her holo-screen at first, pointedly ignoring you for several uncomfortable seconds before shuddering out a long, heavy sigh. The matronly kaithrit leans forward, resting her temples in her hands, still not meeting the gaze you’ve been training on her since you sat down, expectant.
\n\n“So,” she finally says, shielding her eyes behind her laced fingers. “This... is not a conversation I wanted to have.”
\n\nYou neither.
Part of you wants to suggest if that’s the case, you should both forget all about it. But you think better of it, and hold your snark back for once.

\n\nShade takes a deep breath, holds it a moment, and lets it go until her chest looks ready to collapse. Finally, she runs a hand through her silvered hair and looks up at you. Her face is unreadable. Cold. Tired. You can’t tell if she’s angry or sad or just done with you. “I always thought you turned up on a hell of a night, kiddo. First good turn I’d had in this dump... should have known it was a lie. A good one, too. Must have been, since I fell hook, line, and sinker for you. What I don’t get is why. Why’d you go all the way with it; with me? Was this all some kind of sick game to you, or...”
\n\nShe makes something between a laugh and a sigh and turns away, staring off into the crowd for a long while. Looks like she’s waiting for an answer. Your explanation. Why did you sleep with her?

\n\nYou laugh off the near-collision and say that you just found one of your dad’s probes, loaded with the coordinates you need for the next leg of your quest. You might not be around Myrellion much after this, you add, hoping to provoke some decision from the thus-far reticent huntress.
\n\nLuckily, it doesn’t look like your meeting was a coincidence. Shade nods and plants a heavy, firm hand on your shoulder. <i>“Good for you, kid. Looks like I’m off, too.

Gonna head back to Uveto.”
\n\nYour gut forms a knot the moment she says it. No reunion, no forgiveness. Just her... vanishing?

\n\n“Uh...” Shade blinks, staring at the probe. “Did I do something?”
\n\nOne of the Steele Tech pilots jogs over, pulling his Codex back out. “Hey, weren’t these things only supposed to turn on for you, "+pc.mf("Mr.","Ms.")+" Steele?”

You and Shade both look at him expectantly, but rather than assuring you it’s just some random bug in the century-old probe, he pushes another button and turns the scanner towards Shade.
\n\n“Hey,” Shade scowls. “What gives?”

\n\nThe knot that’s been sitting in your stomach only gets tighter as the pilot’s Codex beeps at him, its screen flashing in his face. Despite your distance of late, you reach over and take your lover’s hand, assuring her it’s probably just a bug. She doesn’t object to the gesture, which for a second, puts your mind at ease.
